title: T5
description: An encoder-decoder Transformer checkpoint that framed many language tasks as text-to-text generation from one unified architecture.
openingSummary: T5 matters because it gave readers a clean encoder-decoder checkpoint that turns many language tasks into one text-to-text interface, making it easier to see how sequence-to-sequence framing sits on top of a reusable two-stack Transformer backbone.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: T5 is a research checkpoint from Google that treats many NLP tasks as text in, text out. It is a text-only encoder-decoder Transformer: the encoder reads the source sequence, the decoder generates the target sequence, and both stacks reuse the familiar attention-plus-feed-forward block recipe.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: The input is tokenized text that describes the task and source content in one sequence. The output is another token sequence generated by the decoder. That makes T5 a strong checkpoint page for readers who want to compare plain classification-style encoders with systems that always respond through text generation.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: T5 uses the Transformer family in an encoder-decoder layout. The encoder can read the full source sequence, while the decoder stays autoregressive and consults encoder memory through cross-attention. That makes T5 the natural checkpoint bridge to the encoder-decoder and sequence-to-sequence category pages.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The key module story is still standard attention and feed-forward composition, but spread across two cooperating stacks instead of one causal stack. Cross-attention matters here because it is the bridge that lets the decoder use encoder memory while generating the target sequence.

training.title: Training
training.body: Public T5 summaries emphasize large-scale text pretraining and a text-to-text task framing. In this bounded atlas slice, structured training-regime, dataset, and paper relationship fields remain optional, so the page keeps explicit unavailable states where the registry has not yet been expanded.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: T5 is useful here because it separates architectural questions from product packaging. It shows why sequence-to-sequence systems often use encoder-decoder backbones and why a model can still decode autoregressively even when it is not decoder-only.
